q: Tell me what you know about the Apollo NASA missions? How many there were?
a:
BASED ON PROVIDED DOCUMENTS:

    The documents describe the Apollo 11 mission as achieving the basic mission of the Apollo program: landing two men on the lunar surface and returning them safely to Earth. (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 0)
    Apollo 11 deployed three basic experiment packages on the lunar surface, collected lunar material samples, and took surface photographs; two of the experiments were part of the early Apollo scientific experiment package. (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 0)
    It was the fifth manned flight of the Command and Service Modules and the third manned flight of the Lunar Module. (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 1)
    The single primary mission objective for Apollo 11 was to perform a manned lunar landing and return safely to Earth. In addition, there were 11 secondary objectives within two general categories: (1) to perform selenological inspection and sampling, and (2) to obtain … (the document begins to list these but the excerpt is cut off). (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 2)

INFORMATION WHICH IS CONTRADICTORY IN DOCUMENTS:

    The documents do not contain any conflicting information about Apollo 11. No contradictions are present across the cited sources.

INFORMATION NOT FOUND IN DOCUMENTS:

    The documents do not state how many Apollo missions there were in total. They only discuss Apollo 11 (the mission described).
    A complete list or count of all Apollo missions beyond Apollo 11 is not provided in the supplied documents.
    A full, explicit enumeration of the 11 secondary objectives is not shown in the supplied excerpts.
r: The Apollo 11 mission landed two men on the moon. The documents provided do not give information about the total number of Apollo missions.

q: Did Armstrong participate in the Apollo missions? How else?
a:
BASED ON PROVIDED DOCUMENTS:

    Yes. The Apollo 11 mission crew list shows Armstrong as “Armstrong, Commander; Michael Collins, Command Module Pilot; and Edwin E. Aldrin, Jr., Lunar Module Pilot.” This confirms Armstrong participated in Apollo 11 as the mission Commander. (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 1)
    The documents also state that Armstrong was in the lunar module, in Eagle, during the mission: “Armstrong is now in the lunar module, in Eagle, and the LM activation and checkout appears to be going along very well somewhat ahead of schedule.” (Source: NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 1)

INFORMATION WHICH IS CONTRADICTORY IN DOCUMENTS:

    No conflicting information found about Armstrong in these sources.

INFORMATION NOT FOUND IN DOCUMENTS:

    The documents do not mention any other Apollo missions Armstrong may have participated in.
    The documents do not provide a full list or enumeration of Armstrong’s roles across any missions beyond what is shown for Apollo 11.
r: The crew included Armstrong, Commander; Michael Collins, Command Module Pilot; and Edwin E. Aldrin, Jr., Lunar Module Pilot.

q: What emergency events happened during the Apollo 13 mission?
a:
BASED ON PROVIDED DOCUMENTS:

    'the pressure rapidly dropped in the service module liquid oxygen tank number 2' [DOC apollo 13 AS13_PAO_textract_full_text public affairs officer 2].
    'fuel cells 1 and 3 failed shortly thereafter' [DOC apollo 13 AS13_PAO_textract_full_text public affairs officer 2].
    'A decision was made to scrub any attempt at landing and con- tinue on a lunar fly-by deep space abort type mission' [DOC apollo 13 AS13_PAO_textract_full_text public affairs officer 2].

INFORMATION WHICH IS CONTRADICTORY IN DOCUMENTS:

    None of the provided documents contain conflicting statements about these events.

INFORMATION NOT FOUND IN DOCUMENTS:

    The documents do not state the cause of the liquid oxygen tank pressure drop or the failures of fuel cells 1 and 3.
    The documents do not provide timelines (exact times) for when the pressure drop and fuel cell failures occurred.
r: Some emergencies were a rapid drop in pressure in a liquid oxygen tank and fuel cells one and three failing.

q: How long was the Apollo 11 mission?
a:
BASED ON PROVIDED DOCUMENTS:

    'CDT 10:32 - GET 146:00 ... This is Apollo Control at 146 hours.' [DOC a11transcript_pao_textract_full_text public affairs officer 0]
    'GET 168:28 ... This is Apollo Control at 168 hours, 28 minutes.' [DOC a11transcript_pao_textract_full_text public affairs officer 2]
    'Apollo 11 will leave the moon's sphere of influence at an elapse time of 148 hours 7 minutes 23 seconds.' [DOC apollo 11 NASA_NTRS_Archive_19710015566_textract_full_text nasa archive 1]

INFORMATION WHICH IS CONTRADICTORY IN DOCUMENTS:

    None of the provided documents contain conflicting statements about elapsed times.

INFORMATION NOT FOUND IN DOCUMENTS:

    The documents do not state the total mission duration (launch-to-splashdown time) or an official final mission elapsed time. Therefore I cannot answer how long the Apollo 11 mission was using only the provided documents.

If you can provide additional documents that state the mission's total duration, I can extract the answer from them.
r: The documents do not contain precise information on the duration of the whole mission.

q: How long did the Apollo 11 and Apollo 13 missions take to prepare?
a:
BASED ON PROVIDED DOCUMENTS:

    Apollo 11: 'A normal time line for preparations for EVA would be approximately 2 hours.' [DOC a11transcript_pao_textract_full_text public affairs officer 1]

    Apollo 13: the provided Apollo 13 documents do not state any duration for mission or EVA preparations. The documents do include timeline references (e.g., 'Flight plan now showing rest period for the command module pilot to begin at 116 hours, about an hour and a half ago.' [DOC apollo 13 AS13_PAO_textract_full_text public affairs officer 0] and 'according to the revised flight plan at 112 hours...' [DOC apollo 13 AS13_PAO_textract_full_text public affairs officer 2]) but none give a preparation-time value.

INFORMATION WHICH IS CONTRADICTORY IN DOCUMENTS:

    None of the provided documents conflict on this point.

INFORMATION NOT FOUND IN DOCUMENTS:

    Any stated duration of preparations for Apollo 13 (overall mission preparation or EVA preparation).
    Any overall mission preparation durations (pre-launch planning, training, or countdown preparations) for either Apollo 11 or Apollo 13 beyond the single EVA-prep time given for Apollo 11.
r: The documents do not give a total preparation time for the Apollo 11 and Apollo 13 missions.
